findings.problem: The chapter opens with four consecutive paragraphs re-establishing the Manor routine, the assignment, and Malfoy's reliable presence before the live scene begins. By chapter 5, and with the provided context packs, this reads as premise reorientation rather than fresh pressure; the chapter-specific engine does not arrive until "The argument started over one word."
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:9, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:11. Compress the routine material to one or two paragraphs and make at least one sentence point directly toward this day's altered dynamic or impending conflict before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:13. Keep the tea/library/domestic rhythm only where it is doing chapter-specific work, not re-briefing baseline conditions.
findings.acceptance_test: In revised text anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:3 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:13, a cold reader can identify this chapter's new local pressure before or at the transition into the Latin dispute, and the opening no longer spends four consecutive paragraphs only restating existing Manor-routine facts.

findings.problem: The chapter treats Hermione's proposed protocol change as a charged end-hook, but the local procedure is too shorthand for that weight. "No wand buffer" and "direct contact on the Mark itself" tell the reader that the exam changes, but not cleanly what the current protocol is, what the buffer has been filtering, or why tomorrow's version is more revealing and more intimate.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:353, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:359, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:371. Add one or two concrete lines that define the existing wand-mediated exam, what the buffer currently prevents or blurs, and what sustained palm contact will change in observable terms. Keep Hermione's concealed motive concealed from Draco, but do not keep the procedure itself obscure to the reader.
findings.acceptance_test: From the revised passage anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:349 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:361, a cold reader can state what the old exam did, what tomorrow's exam changes, what Hermione hopes to learn from the change, and what added risk or exposure makes Draco pause before agreeing.

findings.problem: Harry's scene is strongest when he pressures Hermione through sleep, lying, and the house's effect on her, but the exchange pivots into a Draco check-in and culminates in a balanced mini-speech: "I'm glad... I'm asking... I know you... I know what that house is." That reads more composed than spoken and weakens the boundary-pack beat that Harry's protectiveness here should stay anchored to Hermione's welfare rather than a separate Malfoy audit.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:247, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:251, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:265, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:269. Keep Harry's pressure focused on Hermione's exhaustion, the moving house, and the fact that she is lying. If Draco stays in the scene, reduce it to a brief practical probe instead of a second axis of interrogation, and roughen Harry's pressure with blunt repetition, interruption, or ordinary phrasing rather than a neat diagnostic mini-speech.
findings.acceptance_test: In revised text anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:231 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:269, Harry's main pressure remains Hermione's condition and the house's effect on her, not an extended Malfoy evaluation; he does not deliver a polished multi-clause diagnosis, and the scene still lands Hermione's lie plus the sleeve-adjustment beat.

findings.problem: The chapter reproduces calibration-only style-bible example lines verbatim. The standalone acknowledgments at these lines match example material ("Mm." and "Yeah."), and the review contract explicitly forbids reusing example lines in manuscript text even when the copied unit is short.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:99 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:195. Replace those acknowledgments with different character-true wording or fold them into fuller turns so no style-bible example line appears verbatim or near-verbatim in the chapter.
findings.acceptance_test: After revision of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:99 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:195, no line in chapter_05 matches any style-bible example line verbatim or near-verbatim on a direct read.

findings.problem: Hermione's private narration around the resonance stays too orderly and too syntactically self-certifying for the style bible's private register. Instead of a faster, more fractured mind visibly rationalizing itself into a bad explanation, the chapter repeatedly reaches for polished certification and negation scaffolds ("That was...", "It was...", "Not because...") that make the interiority feel authored and settled at the exact point it should be fraying.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:157, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:271, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:273, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:319. Keep Hermione's rationalization, but make at least one sentence visibly self-correct or break under pressure, and replace several of the current certification/negation constructions with concrete thought, sensation, or behavior. The passage should read like Hermione talking herself into a compromised explanation, not filing a clean report about it.
findings.acceptance_test: In revised text anchored to the cited lines, at least one sentence shows Hermione revising or interrupting her own thought on the page, and no more than one of the current certification/negation emphasis constructions ("That was...", "It was...", or "Not because...") remains across those targeted passages.

summary: Chapter 05 lands its required state shift and the core collaboration beat, and I found no chapter-breaking continuity contradiction against the provided packs. It still fails across all four lenses because it is not yet pass-level on execution: the opening spends too long reorienting into routine, the direct-contact end-hook is locally under-explained, Harry's lunch scene drifts into an over-composed Draco check, exact style-bible calibration lines are reused, and Hermione's private narration stays more polished and mechanically certifying than her style-bible register supports.
